_description: Actual match outcomes for Spain vs Belgium QF. No crowd/market data was used for pricing this match (by instruction), so no RBP-vs-crowd figures are computable here -- this file records our estimate vs actual outcome and a directional/distance grade only. Full diagnosis in POSTMORTEM.md.

question_results.text: Will Spain advance to the semifinals?
Will the match be tied at halftime?
question_results.note: HT was 1-1 (goals at 30' and 41'). Monte Carlo simulation output, wrong side of a near-even call.

question_results.text: Will Belgium hold a lead at any point in regulation?
Will either team make a substitution at halftime?
question_results.note: First substitution was 55', outside the halftime window. New base-rate panel's first live test, missed.

question_results.text: Will a goal be scored before the first hydration break?
question_results.note: First goal was 30', after the 24' window.

question_results.text: Will a goal be scored after the second hydration break but before end of regulation?
question_results.note: 88' Merino goal. Near-even call, wrong side.

question_results.text: Will both teams score in regulation?
Will Lamine Yamal score or assist a goal?
question_results.note: 0 goals, 0 assists. 3rd straight correct application of the same validated pattern this tournament.

question_results.text: Will Kevin De Bruyne have 1+ shots on target?
question_results.note: He started and played to 86' (1 SOT) -- our participation-probability judgment (0.82) undershot reality after his R16 DNP scare.

question_results.text: Will Romelu Lukaku have 1+ shots on target?
question_results.note: 0 shots, 0 SOT -- his sub-appearance scoring streak (3 straight goals pre-match) ended with a whimper, not a bang. Correctly hedged rather than chased the streak.

question_results.text: Will Spain have 5+ shots on target?
question_results.note: 8 SOT. The regime-coverage shading (down from raw 78%) didn't cost anything -- Spain cleared the bar with room to spare.

question_results.text: Will Belgium have 3+ shots on target?
question_results.note: WORST RESULT OF THE MATCH. Only 2 SOT -- Belgium completely suppressed by Spain's 67.9% possession. The same regime-coverage check applied to Q11 (Spain's own SOT) was never applied symmetrically here. See POSTMORTEM.md SYMMETRIC_REGIME_COVERAGE.
Only 5 corners despite 17 shots and 67.9% possession -- a genuinely surprising miss given the dominance, not obviously explainable from data on hand.

Exactly 4 cards (2-2), 3 of the 4 shown in the second half incl. two in stoppage time. Matched Smarkets' 0.2976 almost exactly -- both wrong together, a shared blind spot on late-match discipline in tense knockout games.

question_results.text: Will a penalty kick be awarded OR a red card be shown?
question_results.note: No penalty, no reds. Referee-specific data (Oliver's 0 reds in 3 prior matches) held up.

key_lessons: Biggest miss: Belgium 3+ SOT at 0.85, missed by 0.85 -- the SYMMETRIC_REGIME_COVERAGE gap documented in POSTMORTEM.md. The opponent-quality shading applied correctly to Spain's own SOT prop (Q11) was never applied to Belgium's, despite the same underlying fact (Spain's dominance) being known at pricing time.

Monte Carlo simulation (Q2/Q3/Q5/Q6) split 2-2 with both misses near-50/50 pre-match -- statistically unremarkable, not evidence the method is flawed. Initial read blaming the simulation specifically did not survive checking the actual settlement data.

Q14 (cards) matched Smarkets almost exactly (0.30 vs 0.2976) and both were wrong -- independent agreement between model and market is not itself protection against a shared blind spot (late-match discipline in tense knockout games).

Q9 (De Bruyne SOT): the participation-probability judgment (0.82) was the weaker link, not the conditional-rate calculation -- he played virtually the whole match. A single R16 DNP was probably weighted too heavily against the base-rate expectation that a player features in his team's biggest game of the tournament.
